steps.title: Apply the zero product property
steps.content:
The equation $(v - 14)(v - 5) = 0$ is already factored. By the zero product property, when a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero:
$$v - 14 = 0 \quad \text{or} \quad v - 5 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve each factor equation
steps.content:
• From $v - 14 = 0$: $v = 14$
  • From $v - 5 = 0$: $v = 5$
The solutions are $5$ and $14$. That confirms D is correct.
